description:
Consensus High (distributed + testing). On --max-concurrency > 1, a per-site worker killed out-of-band (supervisor shutdown, :kill, OOM, Ctrl-C teardown) skips its with_restore after-block, so the original binary is never reloaded; the {:exit, reason} fold branch aborts honestly but does NO compensating BeamCache sweep. The module stays loaded as its MUTANT and, under ordered: true, detection is deferred while other concurrent workers (and any post-run code in the same BEAM) execute against mutated code -> mis-classified kill/survive.

Trigger: --max-concurrency 2+, any out-of-band kill of a worker.
Blast radius: one scoped module stays mutated; concurrent sites mis-classify; embedded/iex hosts keep the poison after the run.

WHY P1 (despite CF2a gating this path experimental): a module left loaded as its MUTANT is PERSISTENT, process-global state corruption that outlives the mutagen invocation and silently poisons every subsequent compile/test in that BEAM (an iex -S mix session, a watch-mode loop). That footgun warrants a fix even on an experimental path. Scope the fix to "prevent a left-mutant", NOT general parallel robustness.

File: lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:534-547 (async_stream, ordered:true), :577-585 (fold {:exit} branch), :888-905 (with_restore), :1469-1502 (restore/2).

## Fix
On the {:exit, reason} fold branch, sweep ALL scoped modules through BeamCache.restore/3 before returning the abort. The snapshot ETS table is still alive (execute runs inside run_with_beam_cache try; the `after BeamCache.delete` at :230-234 has NOT fired). Sweeping ALL modules is safe and idempotent (restore reloads the snapshot binary a module already has; un-snapshotted modules return {:not_snapshotted} = success).

CRITICAL ORDERING (review finding H1 -- do NOT skip): with ordered:true the {:exit} for site i surfaces while sibling sites i+1..i+N-1 are STILL EXECUTING (each mid mutate/compile/ExUnit on its own mutant). A sweep that runs concurrently with live siblings can :code.load_binary the original over a module a sibling is currently testing its mutant on -> that sibling silently tests ORIGINAL code and scores a spurious "survived", corrupting the very report the abort is failing honestly. THEREFORE: the abort path must FIRST drain / tear down all sibling per-site tasks (await/shutdown the run task-supervisor children) and only AFTER teardown completes perform the restore sweep. The sweep must be strictly ordered after sibling teardown, never concurrent with it. Treat any restore failure during the sweep as part of the same unrecoverable error.

## Test (must be non-vacuous -- review finding H2)
The existing parallel harness uses synthetic, never-snapshotted modules, so restore is a no-op {:not_snapshotted} that returns :ok WHETHER OR NOT the fix swept anything -- an "assert module restored" check on it passes against both fixed and unfixed code (vacuous). Instead:
- Inject a code_server stub whose get_object_code/1 returns a real {binary, filename} for the scoped module (so it IS snapshotted) and whose load_binary/3 RECORDS its calls.
- max_concurrency: 2 + a compiler/process stub that kills its worker on site 1.
- Assert {:error, :unrecoverable_restore_failure, %{message: msg}} with msg =~ "per-site outer task exited".
- Assert the stub recorded a load_binary for the killed-worker scoped module on the {:exit} branch (the sweep actually restored it), AND that the sweep ran after sibling teardown (no interleave with a still-running sibling).

## Done
- Killed parallel worker leaves NO mutated module loaded.
- Restore sweep is ordered strictly after sibling-task teardown (no concurrent restore-vs-mutate interleave).
- New crash test is non-vacuous (recording code_server stub on a snapshotted module) and green; existing serial tests unaffected.

comments.text: WORKER_ERROR: mix spec.check fails on out-of-scope mutagen.ast grep and mutagen.cli archive telemetry verification targets; focused parallel test and mix test passed.

PARENT EPIC BRANCH RENAMED (2026-06-23): merge-back target epic/33b -> mutagen-33b/tier1-review-hardening. This stage's own branch/worktree are unchanged (mutagen-33b.1/restore-on-exit @ ../mutagen_ex-restore-on-exit). Upstream tracking repointed.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-59ca1479
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-restore-on-exit/.codex-runs/mutagen-33b.1.log

Commit: 78318fe9a57c3651ae198c597608cb45e9e2a5a6
Files touched: lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex; test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs
Tests added: MutagenEx.MutationRunnerParallelTest test outer task crash restore sweep worker exit tears down siblings before restoring snapshotted scoped modules/1
Verification stubs flipped: None (mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v7 was already execute: true)

Summary: The parallel outer-task exit path now halts with a sentinel, tears down all sibling Task.Supervisor children, waits for the supervisor to drain, and only then sweeps every scoped module through BeamCache.restore/3. The new crash test uses a snapshotted real victim module with a recording code_server stub, forces one worker to die while a sibling is live, and asserts restore happens after sibling teardown with no live supervisor children.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 3b779503-5d89-4838-8350-946f2c3304f0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/3b779503-5d89-4838-8350-946f2c3304f0.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2 of v2..v9 directly relevant (v7 parallel, v9 beam_cache) + v2/v5/v6, passed: all. Full suite: 641 tests, 0 failures.
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r15 (parallel dispatch, ordered fold, byte-identical aggregate): covered by mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs (7 tests incl. new crash test at :502). New {:exit}-path correctness covered by :502. Would fail if the outer-task-exit fold branch left a scoped module loaded as its mutant (no restore sweep) or restored concurrently with a live sibling.
  - r6 (restore via BeamCache.restore through code_server seam): covered by beam_cache_test.exs + the new test's load_binary recording. Would fail if restore bypassed the snapshot table or skipped load_binary.
- Non-vacuousness of crash test: PASS. The test snapshots a REAL victim module (compile_snapshot returns a genuine BEAM binary; RecordingCodeServer.get_object_code returns {module,binary,filename} so prime_beam_cache inserts a real ETS entry), then asserts RecordingCodeServer.load_calls() contains exactly one recorded load_binary for the killed-worker scoped module with the snapshot binary/filename pinned. EMPIRICALLY PROVEN: I temporarily stubbed abort_after_outer_task_exit to skip restore_all_scoped_modules; the new test FAILED with load_calls() == [] (right: []). Restored source via git checkout. The assertion load-bears the actual sweep.
- Ordering (sweep after sibling teardown): verified two ways. (a) CrashCompilerStub's crash worker calls wait_for_blocking_sibling and only kills itself AFTER blocking_started? flips true, so the blocking sibling is a LIVE Task.Supervisor child at crash time (asserted via events containing {:blocking_worker_started,_}). (b) RecordingCodeServer.load_binary captures Task.Supervisor.children(task_sup) at call time and the test asserts live_children: []. A concurrent or pre-teardown sweep would see the live blocking sibling and the [] match would fail. Source trace confirms: {:exit,reason} fold halts with sentinel {:outer_task_exit, reason, parallel_task_sup} (NO restore in fold); post-reduce case dispatches abort_after_outer_task_exit/3 which teardown_task_supervisor_children + await_task_supervisor_drained THEN restore_all_scoped_modules. Strictly ordered.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. restore/2 -> restore_modules/2 extraction is a minimal shared-helper refactor directly serving the all-scoped sweep; no new deps, no unrelated changes.
- Project verification: pass (delta-gated). mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 641/0; mix spec.check reports exactly errors=2 = mutagen.ast (stale grep) + mutagen.cli (archive telemetry.app), both pre-existing baseline failures tracked in mutagen-ua9. NO new findings.

Verdict: PASS

Notes: The drain loop await_task_supervisor_drained caps at 50 attempts x 10ms (~500ms) before proceeding to the sweep regardless; acceptable for the unrecoverable-abort path. Advisory only.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)

zero mechanical or intent-level scope violations, and passing project verification.
Non-vacuousness was empirically proven (sweep removed -> test fails) and sweep-after-teardown
ordering verified two ways. No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Rebased onto epic and merged into mutagen-33b/tier1-review-hardening (249d377). Smoke green on integrated epic (642 tests, 0 failures). Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: CF1: Restore scoped modules on parallel outer-task :exit + worker-crash test
